pattern: Pre-computing fire arrival times and personal arrival times with two BFS passes turns the wait-time question into checking, for each candidate delay, whether the person can still out-race the fire to the safehouse.
hints: First flood-fill the fire to learn the minute it reaches every cell, and separately BFS your own shortest arrival time to every cell ignoring fire.
If you wait d minutes, you reach the safehouse at personTime + d; you survive a cell only if you arrive strictly before the fire (with a tie allowed only at the safehouse itself).
The largest survivable wait is monotonic, so binary search d in [0, big]; handle the unreachable case as -1 and the fire-can-never-reach case as 1e9.

edge_inputs.description: fire walled off so wait is unbounded
smallest open grid with no fire
tie at the safehouse counts as safe

stress.description: random small grids with sparse fire and walls, ensuring open start and end
stress.note: start and end forced to grass; fire/walls sparse so many cases are reachable
